plan.title: Add provider-neutral worker metadata to the agent preamble

narratives.Description: This story updates the canonical dispatch preamble so implementation workers can see which backend and role they were assigned without relying on Grok Build-specific wording. It is independently buildable because it owns the dispatch-envelope contract text and can preserve existing allocation-context fields while adding provider-neutral metadata guidance.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update the source file templates/agent-prompt-preamble.md near the allocation context and sub-agent spawn rules to document provider-neutral worker metadata such as dispatch provider, worker role, and selected backend or routing policy.
2. Verify the preamble contract with tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py assertions that preserve the five-field allocation-context evidence while checking the new backend metadata guidance.
narratives.UserStory: As a dispatched coding worker, I want my prompt to declare my backend and role in provider-neutral terms, so that I can follow the right constraints without depending on one harness name.

items.title: Given a worker prompt includes allocation context, when backend metadata is present, then the preamble explains dispatch provider, worker role, and backend or routing-policy fields without changing the five-field #1378 recognition contract.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a worker prompt includes allocation context, when backend metadata is present, then the preamble explains dispatch provider, worker role, and backend or routing-policy fields without changing the five-field #1378 recognition contract.

items.title: Given a provider-neutral dispatch uses Composer, Grok Build, Cursor/cloud agents, or a future adapter, then the preamble gives each worker the same role-boundary expectations.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a provider-neutral dispatch uses Composer, Grok Build, Cursor/cloud agents, or a future adapter, then the preamble gives each worker the same role-boundary expectations.

items.title: Given a review cycle or postmortem inspects worker output, then the preamble requires the selected backend or routing policy to be audit-visible in the dispatch envelope or status output.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a review cycle or postmortem inspects worker output, then the preamble requires the selected backend or routing policy to be audit-visible in the dispatch envelope or status output.

expected_outputs: agent preamble documents provider-neutral worker metadata without breaking allocation context

references.title: Issue #1531: swarm: opt-in tiered / heterogeneous dispatch (strong-model orchestrator + cheap high-context leaf code-runners)
